name: Parabolic Reflector with Horn Feed
description: Tests designing a prime-focus parabolic reflector with conical horn exciter
prompt: Design a parabolic reflector antenna at 12 GHz with a conical horn feed. Show the geometry. Provide the generated code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets Exciter property on reflector before calling design()
assertions.expected: Exciter property is assigned (hornConical or equivalent horn) on the reflectorParabolic object BEFORE design() is called. NOT design() called first and then Exciter set afterward
design() is called with a reflectorParabolic object as the FIRST argument. NOT with a different reflector type (reflectorCorner, cassegrain, gregorian) or a bare antenna element without a reflector

assertions.description: User's frequency of 12 GHz is used

name: Cassegrain Dual-Reflector System
description: Tests designing a Cassegrain configuration with correct exciter
prompt: Design a Cassegrain dual-reflector antenna at 10 GHz with a conical horn feed. Provide the generated code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

assertions.expected: design() is called with a cassegrain object as the FIRST argument. NOT with a different reflector type (reflectorParabolic, reflectorCorner, gregorian) or a bare antenna element without a reflector

assertions.description: User's frequency of 10 GHz is used

name: Corner Reflector with Dipole
description: Tests corner reflector with dipole exciter and 90-degree corner
prompt: Design a corner reflector antenna at 1 GHz with a dipole feed. Report the corner angle. Provide the generated code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

assertions.expected: design() is called with a reflectorCorner object as the FIRST argument. NOT with a different reflector type (reflectorParabolic, cassegrain, gregorian) or a bare antenna element without a reflector

assertions.description: User's frequency of 1 GHz is used

name: Offset Gregorian for No Blockage
description: Tests offset dual-reflector when user wants no feed blockage
prompt: Design an offset Gregorian reflector at 14 GHz for a satellite application where feed blockage is unacceptable. Provide the generated code.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

assertions.expected: design() is called with a gregorianOffset object as the FIRST argument. NOT with a different reflector type (reflectorParabolic, cassegrain, reflectorCorner) or a non-offset gregorian

assertions.description: User's frequency of 14 GHz is used
